subject: Endocrinology
question: A 40-year-old woman presents with fatigue, weight gain, cold intolerance, and constipation for 6 months. TSH 18 mIU/L (elevated), Free T4 0.6 ng/dL (low). What is the most appropriate treatment?

options.explanation: Primary hypothyroidism (↑TSH, ↓T4) is treated with levothyroxine. Starting dose 1.6 mcg/kg/day. Recheck TSH in 6-8 weeks, adjust by 12.5-25mcg. Take on empty stomach, 30-60 min before breakfast.
T3 monotherapy not standard. Levothyroxine (T4) preferred as it provides stable physiologic levels via peripheral conversion. T3 has short half-life requiring multiple daily dosing.
Methimazole is for HYPERTHYROIDISM (blocks thyroid hormone synthesis). Would worsen hypothyroidism.
RAI destroys thyroid tissue - used for hyperthyroidism or cancer. Would worsen hypothyroidism.
Prednisone not indicated for primary hypothyroidism. Used for subacute (viral) thyroiditis causing transient hyperthyroidism.

educationalObjective: Treat primary hypothyroidism (↑TSH, ↓T4) with levothyroxine 1.6 mcg/kg/day. Recheck TSH in 6-8 weeks.
keyTakeaway: Hypothyroidism: Levothyroxine 1.6 mcg/kg/day on empty stomach. Goal TSH 0.5-2.5. Recheck q6-8 weeks.
